evals.prompt: We have $5M in cash but $2M is in Euros and the Euro is dropping. We also have a $1M payroll next week. What should we do?
evals.expected_output: Should identify the immediate liquidity need (payroll) and the FX risk (Euro dropping). Should suggest a cash positioning review and potential FX hedging or conversion. Should provide a structured Treasury Report with a cash flow forecast and risk management recommendations.
assertions: Identifies payroll as an immediate liquidity priority
Addresses the FX risk of the Euro dropping
Provides specific hedging or conversion recommendations

evals.prompt: We hold 4B NGN earning 3% in a current account while drawing on a 24% overdraft averaging 1.5B. Fix this.
evals.expected_output: Self-funding arbitrage: sweep idle cash against the overdraft first (saves ~21% spread on 1.5B = ~315M/yr), then ladder the residual into T-bills/money market within policy. Check account structure/pooling and why the silos existed.
assertions: Nets the overdraft against idle cash as priority one
Invests residual per an approved instrument ladder
Addresses the structural cause (account silos, no sweeping)

evals.prompt: Design a cash forecasting process for a group with 12 entities, 3 currencies, and chronic forecast misses over 25%.
evals.expected_output: 13-week direct-method rolling forecast per entity consolidated centrally; standard templates, weekly cadence, driver-based receipts (AR aging x collection curve), accountability via entity-level variance scorecards, and central FX netting.
assertions: Implements a 13-week direct method rolling forecast
Standardizes templates and consolidates centrally with FX views
Builds receipts from AR aging and collection history, not hope
Creates a variance accountability loop per entity

evals.prompt: From the attached treasury position (evals/files/treasury_position.csv): compute liquidity runway in days, the 6-month NGN/USD forward rate under covered interest parity, and the NGN cost of hedging the $1,000,000 payable forward versus at spot.
evals.expected_output: Runway 80 days; 6m forward 1,624.39 NGN/USD; hedged cost NGN 1,624,390,244 vs spot 1,500,000,000 - forward premium 8.3%.
assertions: Hedged payable cost approx NGN 1.62 billion
Explains the forward premium follows from the interest differential (CIP)
